headline: GOLD SURGES TO $4,867 — FOURTH STRAIGHT WEEKLY GAIN AS DOLLAR CRUMBLES, SAFE HAVEN BIDS PILE IN
snippet: Spot gold advanced as much as 1.7% to $4,887 on April 17, its highest level since March, extending a fourth consecutive weekly gain as dollar weakness and geopolitical safe-haven demand converged. Prices have risen more than 25% since early 2025, with the World Gold Council projecting official-sector buying of up to 900 tonnes for full-year 2026.

headline: LME METALS INDEX HITS ALL-TIME RECORD: 'ALUMINUM BLACK HOLE' FEAR AND COPPER REVIVAL DRIVE HISTORIC SURGE
snippet: The London Metal Exchange's benchmark metals gauge hit an all-time record high on April 17, with aluminum surging roughly 15% since the start of Middle East hostilities — Gulf smelter strikes and Strait of Hormuz disruptions squeezing supply — while copper added about 11% over the same period. The index, tracking six primary base metals, has advanced nearly 12% in four weeks, its sharpest run in recorded history.

headline: SILVER CLOSES IN ON $80/OZ, UP 143% IN ONE YEAR — BIGGEST ANNUAL SURGE IN A DECADE
snippet: Silver reached $79.60 per troy ounce on April 17, marching toward one-month highs and registering a staggering 143% year-over-year gain — the metal's strongest annual performance since 2011. The move reflects compounding tailwinds: safe-haven buying alongside gold, industrial demand from solar panel manufacturing, and structural import appetite from China at over 790 tonnes in early 2026.

headline: AMERICAS GOLD & SILVER POSTS RECORD Q1: 787,000 OZ SILVER PRODUCED, UP 76% YEAR-ON-YEAR
snippet: Americas Gold and Silver (TSX: USA) announced record consolidated Q1 2026 silver production of 787,000 ounces on April 16 — a 76% jump from Q1 2025 — driven by Idaho's Galena Complex (424,686 oz) and Cosalá operations in Mexico (362,239 oz), with a record 830,000 oz sold in the quarter. The company guides 3.2–3.6 million ounces of silver for full-year 2026 at an AISC of US$30–35/oz.

headline: GDX UP 103% IN ONE YEAR — GOLD MINING STOCKS FINALLY OUTRUNNING THE METAL AS VALUATIONS CATCH UP
snippet: Throughout most of 2025 gold equities badly lagged the metal — spot gold rose over 60% while GDX gained only 35–40% — but 2026 has brought a structural reversal, with VanEck Gold Miners ETF now up over 103% year-on-year as record margins and a wave of capital returns force a valuation reset. The analysis argues this catch-up trade has further to run, with mid-tier producers generating unprecedented free cash flow at $4,800+ gold and still trading at discounts to intrinsic NAV.

headline: SPROTT LAUNCHES FIRST EX-CHINA RARE EARTH ETF (REXC) AS WESTERN SUPPLY CHAIN BATTLE INTENSIFIES
snippet: Sprott Inc. launched the Sprott Rare Earths Ex-China ETF (Nasdaq: REXC) on April 14 — the only ETF offering pure-play exposure to rare earth miners, separators and refiners operating outside China — tracking the Nasdaq Sprott Rare Earths Ex-China Index and investing at least 80% of assets in ex-China companies. The launch comes as China increasingly uses its rare earth refining dominance as a geopolitical lever, with competing VanEck REMX ETF having grown from under $1B to $3B AUM in just 12 months.

headline: ANALYST WARNING: USA RARE EARTH'S $1.6B GOVERNMENT DEAL CARRIES A HIDDEN DILUTION TRAP
snippet: A Motley Fool analysis published April 17 argues that the U.S. government's letter of intent for a $1.6B equity stake in USA Rare Earth (USAR) is not a guaranteed funding commitment — Washington keeps its equity even if funding never materializes, diluting existing shareholders for nothing in return. The author says the critical minerals arms race has cleaner plays with better-secured capital structures and advises retail investors to look elsewhere.

headline: CONGO RAISES US COPPER COMMITMENT FIVEFOLD TO 500,000T AS DRC PIVOTS HARD AWAY FROM CHINA
snippet: DRC state miner Gécamines has committed 500,000 tonnes of copper for US-bound sale — a fivefold jump from its January pledge of 100,000 tonnes — with material sourced from CMOC's Tenke Fungurume mine and routed through Swiss trader Mercuria. The strategic pivot signals a significant reorientation of DRC copper supply away from Chinese buyers, driven by U.S. critical minerals policy pressure and an effort to monetize state mining assets through Western partnerships.

headline: LITHIUM CARBONATE NEARLY DOUBLES IN Q1 2026 AS ENERGY STORAGE BOOM REWRITES THE DEMAND STORY
snippet: Battery-grade lithium carbonate surged from roughly US$13,433/tonne in early December to $26,278 by late January — nearly doubling in under two months — as Chinese supply delays and speculative buying converged with a structural demand surge driven by grid-scale energy storage. Grid storage now accounts for 15% of total lithium demand and is expanding at 55% annually, with a meaningful supply response expected to lag by 12–18 months.

headline: NEXGEN EYES SUMMER 2026 GROUNDBREAKING ON ROOK I — THE BIGGEST URANIUM MINE EVER BUILT IN CANADA
snippet: NexGen Energy (TSX: NXE), following final CNSC federal approval in March 2026, plans to break ground on the Rook I uranium mine in northern Saskatchewan this summer — the first new Canadian uranium mine construction authorization since 2004. At full run-rate, Rook I could produce up to 30 million pounds of uranium annually, exceeding 50% of total Western uranium supply capacity, with first production targeted for approximately 2030.
